The two were very close and had a strong friendship. However, as “Reality Check” shows, their relationship hit a pause after Manuel wanted to leave the show after Season 17. In the docuseries, he expressed how the show’s direction was changing and he wanted to pursue other projects.
He said that he sent Banks an email thanking her for the opportunity but wanted leave.
"She didn’t respond. It was probable the longest three days ever,” Manuel said in the documentary. “And she ultimately wrote back just three words: I am disappointed.”
He said that “all communication stopped between” him and Banks.
When asked to comment on their friendship, Banks said, “Nah. I’d prefer... I... Yeah, I should call Jay. I don’t want to do this here. But he’s a special man.”
Manuel did one more season before he, Miss Jay and Nigel Barker were fired.
Following the show, Manuel worked with E!, covering red carpets. He also launched a cosmetics company “Jay Manuel Beauty Retail Experience,” and wrote a book, “The Wig, the Bitch and the Meltdown.”
Miss Jay
Jay Alexander, also known as Miss Jay, was the show's runway coach and judge. He taught the models, including Banks, how to perfect their runway walk.
He and Banks knew each other since she was 16 and revered by the fashion industry. He was on the show for 18 seasons before being let go. He returned as a judge for Season 21 and 22.
Miss Jay would go on to work on other “America’s Next Top Model” versions around the world. In “Reality Check,” he reveals that on Dec. 27, 2022, he suffered a stroke.
He spent five weeks in a coma and couldn't walk or talk.

“I miss being the queen of the runway, the catwalks, of course. I’m the person who taught models how to walk. I taught models how to walk and now I can’t walk. Not yet. Not yet. I’m determined to walk. I’m sure you’re going to see me again. I’m sure it’s not over for me yet,” he said.
While in the hospital, Manuel and photographer Nigel Barker visited him, while Banks did not.
Nigel Barker
Another popular figure in “America’s Next Top Model“ was photographer Nigel Barker. The creative joined the show in Season 2 as a judge and photographer.

In “Reality Check” Barker shares how he joined the team as the “straight man eye candy.” He was a part of some of the most iconic and controversial shoots until Season 18, when he was also fired. He returned as a guest for the show's final season.

After "ANTM," Barker worked with Taylor Swift, the Miss Universe  and Miss USA competitions. He also released a couple of fashion and photography books, including "Models of Influence: 50 Women Who Reset the Course of Fashion."
He is also the co-founder of The Barker Company, an alcohol brand that created it's own espresso martini.
Janice Dickinson

Supermodel Janice Dickinson was an original judge on the show from Season 1 to Season 4. For Season 5 to 7, she returned as a guest and recurring judge. Kimora Lee Simmons and Beau Quillian were also original judges but only for Season 1.
Dickinson was known for her harsh critiques of the contestants and butting heads with Banks over their opinions about the models.
While Dickinson is not featured in “Reality Check,” she does appear in another upcoming E! docuseries, “Dirty Rotten Scandals.”

After “ANTM,” she would go on to appear in other reality shows like “Celebrity Big Brother,” “House of Villains,” “The Dark Side of Reality TV” and “I’m a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here,” among other shows. These days, the 80s supermodel critiques people's runway walks on her Instagram.

title: Read the Beginning of 'Laws of Love and Logic,' a New Book From Jenna's Imprint
summary: Debra Curtis says one question "absolutely consumes" Lily Webb, the protagonist of her debut novel, "Laws of Love and Logic."What if
story: Debra Curtis says one question "absolutely consumes" Lily Webb, the protagonist of her debut novel, "Laws of Love and Logic."
What if?
The book, out from Jenna Bush Hager's Thousand Voices x Random House Publishing Group imprint, is an "epic love story about a woman torn between her first love and her husband," Jenna said on TODAY.
Curtis, 62, is making her fiction debut. She is a retired professor of cultural anthropology at Salve Regina University, where she specialized in gender and sexuality.
Speaking to TODAY.com, Curtis said the book "feels like a long conversation I’ve been having with myself for 30 years."

While reading, Curtis said she hopes people ask themselves: "Could I ever truly forgive the unforgivable? If all life is interconnected, does it require us to love differently?And for female readers especially: how am I complicit, even unintentionally, in the subordination of other women?"

"Laws of Love and Logic" took Curtis 10 years to write, and the impact of this moment is not lost on her. "Given that I received well over a hundred rejections from literary agents, it still feels a bit unbelievable," she said.
Below, read the opening of the book.
Read the Beginning of 'Laws of Love and Logic'

Early fall. Leaves of gold on the birch shimmered when the wind picked up. When no one answered the front door, Lily walked around the side of the house to the back deck. She could hear her boyfriend’s father playing guitar and singing softly, a melancholy tune—­ familiar, but she couldn’t quite make it out. The boy, like his dad, was musically inclined. Playing the guitar had a calming effect on Mr. Cooper, especially after his son’s football games.

The back deck was unfinished, littered with tools and cans of paint with rusted lids—­ neglected since last spring—­ alongside a spare tire, two fishing rods, and a cooler. The boy’s dad was on his seventh beer; by the time the day was over he would have finished off a case by himself. Lily didn’t want to disturb Mr. Cooper and waited until he looked up from his guitar.
“There’s my favorite girl,” he said with a big smile, resting his forearm on the body of the instrument.
“Don’t stop on account of me,” Lily said. “I love hearing you play.”
Mr. Cooper, still smiling, shook his head and leaned his guitar against a post on the deck.
“Some game, huh?” he said as he stood up.
He offered Lily his seat, the only one on the deck: a mustard-­ and-orange-­webbed aluminum lawn chair. When she refused to sit, he remained standing.

It had been an exciting game that morning: Portsmouth against Barrington, rival teams. The boy, a senior at Portsmouth and the team’s quarterback, had read the defenses instantly, successfully changing the play at the line of scrimmage three times when Barrington’s defense had anticipated Portsmouth’s plays and was prepared to stop them.

When the boy appeared at the screen door in a pair of jeans and a Creedence Clearwater Revival T-­ shirt, toweling off his long brown hair, his dad couldn’t contain himself and gave the boy a hug, patting his back.
“Four hundred and ninety passing yards! That’s a record. One game! That’s my boy!” Mr. Cooper was smiling from ear to ear, so excited that he spat as he talked.
The boy watched Lily. She never seemed to mind that his father always drank and talked endlessly about the boy’s football stats.
“Everybody knows he’s the best the state has ever seen. When he was fourteen and there were sixty seconds left on the clock, he threw the ball fifty yards and that Portuguese kid caught it.”
“Lil knows the story,” the boy said, putting his arm around his dad. He was tall like his father but a whole lot bigger.
“I was there, Mr. Cooper—­ I saw the whole thing. Just like Roger Staubach!”
“Marry this girl!” the boy’s father said. He took a long swig of his beer.

The boy knew that over the past five decades, the little state of Rhode Island had produced three professional quarterbacks; he was determined to be the fourth. He was ten years old when Joe Namath predicted that the Jets would beat the Colts in the 1969 Super Bowl.
As a young kid, the boy had borrowed Namath’s courage and bold ness. Sometimes, standing in the locker room in front of his team mates, he would say, “We’re going to win this one, I guarantee it.”

The boy took Lily’s hand, led her inside, and pulled the screen door shut behind him. He took two beers out of the refrigerator, and as he and Lily made their way downstairs to the basement, they heard his father: “Chuck at the hardware store said that the scouts will be coming around to have a look at you!”

When the boy was six, his mom took off and moved to Florida with the golf pro from Green Valley golf course. For Christmases and birthdays, she sent her son cards. The boy had learned that his mother was playing piano in a bar in Boca Raton. Back then no one had ever heard of Boca Raton but the boy found it on a map when he was in the second grade. Folks in town heard that the golf pro hit her occasionally and got one of the waitresses pregnant at the resort where he worked.

Every trophy the boy had ever won was displayed on the wooden shelves his father made for him. While football was his main sport, he also played basketball and baseball. The largest trophy was the one he’d received the prior year for football: MVP, a pewter sculpted figure —­ poised to throw a football —­ mounted on a wooden base. Free weights and a bench filled one side of the boy’s basement room, with a bed, a TV, a set of drawers, and an old electric keyboard on the other side. In the corner was a brand-­ new Sony stereo, which his father had recently bought him for his eighteenth birthday. Albums were care fully lined up between two cinder blocks.
Lily placed her beer near a stack of library books on the chest of drawers and picked up the boy’s sunglasses. Putting them on, she stretched out on his bed. The boy sat down at the keyboard.
“Did I ever tell you that my mom played the organ at Saint Barnabas and that’s where she met my dad?”
Lily turned her head on the pillow to watch him. He had told her. this, but she didn’t mind hearing it again, not in the least. “Tell me” was all she said.
“He fell in love with her on a Sunday in between the Gloria and Communion.”
“That’s beautiful.”

“After my mom left, he just couldn’t go back there, so that’s why we go to Saint Anthony’s. I can remember our last Christmas together. Although, I didn’t know it would be our last. She played ‘O Holy Night’ at Mass. I was real sad watching my dad cry.”
Lily swung her legs over the side of the bed and sat up. Before she could say anything, he changed the subject and said, “Do you know this song?”
His hands moved tentatively across the black and white keys.

Like birds pecking in the sand, his fingers bent lightly. He started and stopped a couple of times, but when he was able to pull it together, combining the chords with the right individual notes, Lily exclaimed,
“How did you learn that?”
A little soft, but melodic, the boy sang: “The screen door slams,
Mary’s dress sways / Like a vision she dances . . . ”

As the boy worked out the chords to “Thunder Road,” Lily slipped her hand under his shirt and caressed his back. Born to Run was released the year before, and Lily and the boy both unknowingly bought the other the album for Christmas. The boy stopped playing and looked up at Lily. “That’s as far as I’ve gotten.”
“Call in sick tonight,” Lily said.
“I can’t. You know that.”
“What’s a party without the team quarterback?”
“Are you still planning on going?”

When Lily didn’t answer, the boy rose from the keyboard bench and moved over to his bed. He worked Saturday and Sunday nights, but never Fridays, not the night before a game. He washed dishes at Reidy’s diner on East Main Road. Late into the shift, when it was just the owner and the boy, the owner would fill Styrofoam containers with mashed potatoes, green beans, and meat loaf. The Saturday night special. He’d always add a couple of desserts, one for the boy and one for his father.
The boy sat on the edge of his bed. Lily followed him. She got down on her knees and wiggled herself in between his thighs.
“I promise you’ll have a good time,” Lily said.

She and the boy had been together since they were freshmen, and while they had yet to have sex, they delighted in each other’s caresses with hands and mouths, here in the basement or in the field behind the school. With the tip of her finger, Lily moved a strand of her hair away from her face.
“I don’t understand why you want to go to the party without me,” the boy said.
“Am I supposed to sit home every Saturday night my senior year because you have to work?”
The boy looked away.
“I’m sorry,” she added.

And she was. She knew the boy helped his father out with the basic bills: electricity, groceries, and sometimes the oil bill when the winters were at their worst. The boy had his first job when he was thirteen, a paper route. The factory in Fall River, where his father had worked for fifteen years and planned on working for the rest of his life, had closed down. Now his father earned a living working at one of the marinas on the island. Like many working-­ class kids, the boy was coming of age in a time of great economic anxiety.
“I’ll go with Jane,” Lily said.
“Yeah? And by ten o’clock either you’ll be holding her hair back while she’s puking or she’ll be upstairs in one of the bedrooms with Jimmy Sullivan or some other guy she barely knows.”
“That’s my sister you’re talking about. And what difference does it make who she’s in a bedroom with?”

The boy knew that Lily would go to the party without him. He couldn’t help but feel jealous, so much so that he decided not to tell her what he had been holding in all afternoon—­ something he hadn’t even shared with his father: There had been scouts at the game today.
He would tell her tomorrow. After the party.

Excerpted from LAWS OF LOVE AND LOGIC by Debra Curtis. Copyright © 2026 by Debra Curtis. Excerpted by permission of Ballantine Books, an imprint of Penguin Random House LLC. All rights reserved. No part of this excerpt may be reproduced or reprinted without permission in writing from the publisher.

title: Trailer Released for One of James Van Der Beek's Final Roles Before His Death
summary: In one of his last acting roles before his death, James Van Der Beek takes a turn to the dark side as a murderous pastor in a film set to be released in March.
story: In one of his last acting roles before his death, James Van Der Beek takes a turn to the dark side as a murderous pastor in a film set to be released in March.

Lionsgate offered a sneak peek at Van Der Beek's performance in "The Gates" in a trailer released on Feb. 18. The new trailer came out one week after Van Der Beek's wife announced that the former "Dawson's Creek" star had died at 48 after years of being treated for colorectal cancer.

The upcoming movie also stars Mason Gooding, Keith Powers and Algee Smith as college students having a night out when they take a shortcut to avoid gridlock traffic and end up in a gated community. After being stopped by another gate in the neighborhood, they walk out on foot to ask for help.

The friends then watch through a window as Van Der Beek's character kills a woman inside a home. Van Der Beek sees that they have witnessed his crime and looks to silence them before they can escape the gated community.

The pastor calls on others to find the friends and "let God's will be done." A disheveled-looking Van Der Beek is later seen holding a gun on one of them and telling him, "You've seen what happens when people get in my way."
"The Gates" is set to hit theaters on March 13.
Van Der Beek continued acting right up until the end of his life after announcing his cancer diagnosis in 2024.

The "Varsity Blues" star returned to his football roots with a trio of Tubi movies in 2024 and 2025: "Sidelined: The QB and Me," "Sidelined: Rewind with Noah and Siena," and "Sidelined 2: Intercepted." He also appeared in an episode of "Walker" in 2024 and two episodes of "Overcompensating" in 2025.

"The Gates" is one of two projects in 2026 featuring Van Der Beek that will be released after his death. He also appears in an episode of "Elle," a comedy series that is a prequel to "Legally Blonde."

title: Martha Stewart Gives Brutal Dating Advice to Olympic Snowboarder Who Went Viral for His Valentine Search
summary: Martha Stewart isn't afraid to tell it like it is. On TODAY, she gave brutally honest feedback to snowboarder Nick Baumgartner as his quest for love at the Olympics goes viral.
story: Martha Stewart isn't afraid to tell it like it is.

On Feb. 18, the lifestyle guru offered some rather blunt words of wisdom to American snowboarder Nick Baumgartner, whose Instagram posts about his search for love at the 2026 Winter Olympics have gone viral.
While appearing on TODAY with her pal Snoop Dogg, Stewart came face to face with Baumgartner and got right to it when TODAY's Carson Daly asked her what advice she had for the athlete.
"Oh my gosh, I don't know about Nick," she said as the Olympian sat down beside her. "How about getting rid of the hat?"
Baumgartner, who was sporting a blue fedora hat and an Olympics-themed jacket that read "Coach Snoop," touched his hat and said, "I can't" as the TODAY co-hosts laughed in the background.
"Why?" Stewart asked.
"This is a beautiful hat," he replied.
"I know it is beautiful," Stewart said.
Then, Baumgartner got to the heart of the matter and explained why he really didn't want to ditch his chic accessory.
"Plus, I'm getting old and I'm losing my hair,"
Baumgartner's honesty made Stewart and Snoop Dogg laugh. Then, Stewart dug for some more details about the athlete's love life.
"You're not married?" she asked.
"Not married, no," he said.
"No girlfriend?" she asked.
"No girlfriend," he replied.

He explained that he's been a little busy with the task at hand: competing at the Olympics. Baumgartner competed in men's snowboard cross and mixed team snowboard cross, where he finished in seventh and 15th, respectively.

Amid his events, the athlete shared several comedic posts about his love life. In one short video, he stands looking out at the slopes alone with the text: “Just out here looking for all the wom(e)n my mom said I’d have to fight off when I grew up ‘cause I’m so handsome?”
"Alone for Valentine’s Day again I guess," he added in the caption.
In another post, Baumgartner impersonated Will Ferrell’s “Anchorman” character Ron Burgundy as he explained what he would do if he found a valentine in time for Valentine’s Day.
“Gonna find my baby, gonna hold her tight, gonna have some afternoon delight,” he pretended to sing.
Unfortunately, Baumgartner didn’t find a valentine, but he had some fun poking fun at himself in another video he captioned, “Maybe next year…”
During the TODAY interview Feb. 18, Snoop Dogg also shared his point of view on Baumgartner's quest for love.

"This is what I say, slick Nick. Look, one thing about love, you can't look for it. You've gotta let it find you. You're in Italy right now. And if you're in Italy right now, keep being you. Keep doing what you're doing. I feel like that magnet of love is gonna attract itself right to you," he said.
"Oh man, Snoop's got game," Baumgartner responded. "So I'm gonna trust that."

title: 'Top Model' Winner Adrianne Curry Defends Tyra Banks in a Fiery Video
summary: "America's Next Top Model" Season 1 winner Adrianne Curry defended Tyra Banks
story: "America's Next Top Model" Season 1 winner Adrianne Curry is coming to Tyra Banks' defense after a docuseries about the reality competition series has viewers scrutinizing its creator.
Curry shared a message on her social media accounts, including X and Instagram, on Feb. 17 and said she has "mad respect" for Banks.

"She is not sorry. She is not apologizing to you. That b---- is not f------ sorry. She will not bend the knee and I respect that. What do you want? Her to lie? Lie and say how bad she feels?" Curry said.
The video was a response to some critics of the docuseries, who say that Banks was unapologetic while discussing the way the show treated its contestants.

"Everyone’s coming to me and they’re like, 'None of the judges were sorry.' Why should they be? They’re all loaded. Why should they give a flying f---? You know, if they didn’t give a f--- at the time, they certainly ain’t going to give a f--- now," Curry continued. "But mad respect for Tyra because that’s hard to push back on that many people trying to struggle session you. And she’s just like, 'F--- you and do what I want.'"
Many former “America’s Next Top Model” contestants appear in the new docuseries, which discusses the cultural phenomenon the reality series created, but its Season 1 winner is notably absent.
In January, Curry took to social media to announce that she had opted out of appearing in “Reality Check: Inside America’s Next Top Model." Below, find out what led to her decision.
Why Did Curry Decide Not to Appear in the Docuseries?
On Jan. 26, Curry shared a series of posts on X to explain why she wasn't interested in revisiting the "America's Next Top Model" era.
In her post, she critiqued what she saw to be the documentary's mission of looking back on the show, which started in 2006, through a 2026 lens.
"I am deeply grateful I won the first season of top model. I think people psychoanalyzing it over 20 years later with a woke lens is absurd," she wrote.
Curry went on to to describe why she declined to give an interview.
"I don’t trust people to not manipulate things I say for TV, so I decline everything," she wrote.
Curry said she was also nervous to see how the public would respond to the docuseries.
"The public is cult-like and cruel, so the last thing I want is a bunch of eyeballs on me. I hope the other girls do not have their words twisted in their netflix show," she wrote.
While responding to several comments from her followers, Curry said she has "been burned far too many times by producers to trust any."
Over on Instagram, Curry also shared a video of herself shaking her finger and head overlaid on top of a post from Netflix promoting the docuseries.
"I say no to all of these. I have 0 trust in any producers, no desire to be really public in this day and age.... and am hard retired from Hollywood," she captioned the post.
Where Is Adrianne Curry Now?
Curry now sells Avon products and lives in Montana with her husband, former Twitch streamer Matthew Rhode.
On her Instagram page, Curry describes herself as “Avon’s Baddest Lady” and says she’s “Aging Gracefully Amid Montana’s Peaks.”
On X, the former model said that she “retired from entertainment in 2015 and sought a normal life minus botox, real housewives appearances and clinging to my past.”

title: ‘Love Is Blind’ Star Bri Unpacks What Chris Said During Their ‘Very Shocking’ Conversation
summary: Love Is Blind star Bri reveals what happened during her shocking conversation with Chris and what he told her after breaking up with Jessica.
story: In the newest group of “Love Is Blind” Season 10 episodes, the “pod squad” reunited and one contestant revealed his “shocking” unfiltered thoughts about one couple.
Every iteration of “Love Is Blind” has love triangles that are the source of drama, and the latest Ohio-based season of the hit Netflix dating show is no exception.

In the pods, Season 10 star Bri McNees developed feelings for Connor Spies and Chris Fusco. But as the first part of the experiment progressed, McNees and Fusco mutually decided they had stronger connections with other people. She got engaged to Spies and he proposed to Jessica Barrett.
Both couples seemed to be headed down the aisle until Episode 9, out Feb. 18.
The episode began with Fusco informing a stunned Barrett that he was preoccupied with thoughts about his other connection — McNees — and that he usually dates women who work out every day.

They called off their engagement, and later arrived separately at the “pod squad” meet up toward the end of the episode. There, Fusco had a private conversation with McNees in which he revealed he felt “regret” when he saw her in person.
“‘That is the girl, like, I would literally propose to,’” he said he thought after meeting her. He also called Spies a “submissive” guy and said McNees needs someone more “dominant.”
“It just caught me off guard,” McNees says about Fusco’s comments and demeanor during their conversation in an interview with TODAY.com.

“What was interesting is I think we all felt a shift with Chris, whether that was me, Jess or the entire group that was at that party,” she recalls. “I know that alcohol was involved, so I’m sure emotions were heightened. But I think it all took us by surprise that he was kind of being so forward about just his opinion, whether it was on me or my relationship with Connor.”
She says that she had formed a friendship with Fusco outside of the pods when the engaged contestants met in person in Mexico.
“Connor and Chris were friends up until that point,” she adds. “So it was very surprising, very shocking, and it just caught me off guard.”

McNees continues, “But I think I kind of closed the loop and let him know that I chose Connor, and I continue to stand by that decision. I think it comes across clear that it’s not an open door for me just because he said those things to me.”
As for Spies’ thoughts on the conversation, he tells TODAY.com in a separate interview that he was unaware what Fusco and McNees were talking about at the time.
“I didn’t know that they were talking about that when we were at the bar. I was just off in la la land with the boys having a few drinks and bowling. In the moment, I had no idea,” he says.

The fallout from Fusco’s chat with McNees will unfold in the next group of episodes. Also, it seems McNees and Spies will have a challenging conversation as she is seen asking him, “Think you are ready for marriage?” Episodes 10 and 11 will arrive on Netflix Feb. 25.

title: ‘Love Is Blind’ Season 10 Star Alex Unpacks the 'Pretty Aggressive' Grilling from Ashley's Dad: 'Definitely a Difficult Conversation'
summary: Alex from Love Is Blind Season 10 talks the awkward and aggressive conversation he had with fiance Alex's dad.
story: “Love Is Blind” fans have watched contestants introduce their fiancés or fiancées to their families in every season of the Netflix show, leading to some uncomfortable conversations. But, few have been as intense as the grilling Season 10 star Alex Henderson experienced when he met Ashley Carpenter’s dad.

In Episode 7, out Feb. 18, Henderson and Carpenter, who had continued to get along swimmingly after getting engaged in the pods and vacationing in Mexico, faced their biggest challenge yet. The couple joined Carpenter’s family — dad Paul, mom Jackie and brother Pete — for a dinner that quickly became an interrogation.

Before her family arrived, Carpenter told the cameras that she was “a little nervous” for Henderson to meet her dad specifically. “My dad is the protector. He’s the provider. He will do what it takes to, you know, fight for his family,” she said.

Her dad started off asking Henderson a few simple questions about their connection. Then, for around 10 minutes, the patriarch probed Henderson for information about his past occupations, current job, upbringing, where he has lived, his future plans and political beliefs.
The conversation was just as arduous in person as it came across on television, Henderson reveals to TODAY.com.
“I’ve dated before this show, and I’ve met girlfriends’ parents before. They’ve always been warm and open. This was obviously not the case,” he shares. “It was pretty, pretty aggressive.”

Looking back at the interaction, Henderson says, in the moment, he was still trying to figure out if he wanted to marry Carpenter and if Paul would become his father-in-law. He says he was unsure how to react to the questioning.

“There’s no real way to fully respond with the full scorched-earth policy. But also, how are you going to respond and show that you’re a real person here, and actually, you know, able to stand up for yourself? So it was a pretty thin line for me,” he explains.
Although he had yet to see the second batch of episodes when speaking with TODAY.com, he adds that he is “pleased” with how he handled himself.
“It was a definitely difficult conversation for me,” Henderson says.
Carpenter mostly stayed quiet during her father’s back-and-forth with Henderson in the episode. Her mother and brother also rarely chimed in.
After the dinner ended, Henderson says he wanted to get his fiancée’s perspective on the situation.
Ash didn’t really bring up a ton about it. I kept kind of (asking), ‘What’d your parents think? What was going on? How did it go?’ And it wasn’t really responded to,” he shares.

“So, she kind of just let it go,” he continues. “It did, to me, feel as though that’s normal for their type of dinner table. It’s an interesting dynamic. So it felt as though she was used to that, and didn’t really bring it up.”

The couple dealt with more challenges in Episodes 8, resulting in a lengthy disagreement about their jobs, children and where they would live if they tied the knot. However, it appeared the two had kissed and made up in Episode 9, and Henderson introduced Carpenter to his mom, Kristin.

There will be more issues that arise, though, as the trailer for the next group episodes, arriving Feb. 25, show Henderson and Carpenter arguing again. Viewers will have to wait to see if the two make it down the aisle.

title: What Time Does 'The Night Agent' Season 3 Land on Netflix?
summary: What time does The Night Agent Season 3 come out? Find out what time the new season is available on Netflix.
story: Spoilers ahead for Season 2 of "The Night Agent"
"The Night Agent" is nearly back on Netflix for a third season after a shocking cliffhanger at the end of Season 2.

The show stars Gabriel Basso as Peter Sutherland, a low-level FBI agent tasked with manning a phone that never rings until, one day, it does. Calling in is Rose Larkin (Luciane Buchanan), a tech entrepreneur who provides the code "night action." From there, Peter learns about a secret government agency of the same name and more is uncovered.
At the end of Season 2, Peter agrees to be a double agent in order to have his criminal record cleared. Fans wonder what Peter will be investigating in Season 3 with this new role.
The official trailer for this season showed Peter on a mission outside the United States, indicating this season will follow him on an international mission.
Read on to learn when "The Night Agent" Season 3 will premiere on Netflix in February.
When Does 'The Night Agent' Season 3 Premiere?
"The Night Agent" Season 3 will be available for streaming on Netflix on Feb. 19.
What Time Does 'The Night Agent' Season 3 Come Out?
Season 3 of "The Night Agent" will hit Netflix on Thursday, Feb. 19 at 3 a.m. ET and 12 a.m. PT.
How Many Episodes Will Be In 'The Night Agent' Season 3?
The third season of "The Night Agent" will contain 10 episodes, that will all be released at the same time on Feb. 19.

title: ‘Star Search’ Reboot Crowns Its 1st Winner
summary: Netflix has revealed the first winner of its “Star Search” reboot. Find out who won.
story: Netflix has revealed the first winner of its “Star Search” reboot.

For the past five weeks, host Anthony Anderson has introduced viewers across the world to contestants showing off their vocal chops, dance moves, sleight of hand or other unique skills. After performing live, the contestants received feedback from the three judges — Chrissy Teigen, Jelly Roll and Sarah Michelle Gellar — who rated them on a scale of one to five stars. Then, fans watching at home also voted on the acts in real-time.

Anderson revealed after an intense hour of competition that magician TJ Salta was the winner, taking home the $500,000 prize. He saved his best trick for last, making the judges' rings disappear and reappear in a variety of ways, including being flown in by a bird.

At the start of the finale on Tuesday, Feb. 17, eight acts in eight different categories remained, all hoping to win the cash prize. Some have been entertaining viewers since the season began, and others just earned their place in the finale last week after successfully dethroning the previous champions.
Read on for a recap of the finale, below.
What Happened During the Semi-Finals?
For the first time this season, all of the acts went head-to-head. The second twist: The judges commented on every act, but only the audience could vote on the performances.
Here are the eight finalists and which category they represented:
Dance: Movement 55Music Solo: Spud HowardJunior Variety: Harry Merlin PiperVariety: Duo VespertilioJuniors Music: Madison LaurenMusic Group: H3rizonComedy: Janine HarouniVariety: TJ Salta
Dance: Movement 55
Music Solo: Spud Howard
Junior Variety: Harry Merlin Piper
Variety: Duo Vespertilio
Juniors Music: Madison Lauren
Music Group: H3rizon
Comedy: Janine Harouni
Variety: TJ Salta

Movement 55, who last performed a routine to Lady Gaga’s “Bad Romance,” have been champions for four weeks in a row. Teenage sensation Harry Merlin Piper, 15, has also defeated his challengers four times with his captivating bait and switch magic routine. Acrobats Duo Vespertilio took their aerial routine to another level last week, continuing their four-week winning streak. Trio H3rizon switched it up to secure a spot in the finale by performing the ballad “Free” from “KPop Demon Hunters.” Rounding out the list of four-time champions is magician TJ Salta, who incorporated music into his trick in the semi-finals episode.

Spud Howard challenged Sophie Saylor last week and became the champion thanks to his soulful rendition of “Help!” by The Beatles. Along with Howard, Madison Lauren, 11, also pulled off a surprise upset in the semi-finals and was named the champion after her flawless performance of “A Million Dreams” from “The Greatest Showman.” The final new champion is Janine Harold, whose jokes about her husband proposing to her made the audience laugh.
What Happened During the Finale?
After four head-to-head competitions in the first round of the finale, viewers voted for the winner from the top four. Then, the champion was crowned. Here’s a breakdown of the two rounds.
Round 1:
Madison Lauren receives 3.79 stars.
Harry Merlin Piper receives 4.3 stars.
Winner: Harry Merlin Piper
Duo Vespertilio receive 3.95 stars.
Movement 55 receive 3.67 stars.
Winner: Duo Vespertilio
Spud Howard receives 3.98 stars.
H3rizon receive 3.70 stars.
Winner: Spud Howard
Janine Harouni receives 3.49 stars.
TJ Salta receives 4.32 stars.
Winner: TJ Salta
Round 2:
Harry Merlin Piper
Duo Vespertilio
Spud Howard
TJ Salta
And the winner is...
Fourth place: Duo Vespertilio
Third place: Spud Howard
Second place: Harry Merlin Piper
First place: TJ Salta
After hearing he was named the "Star Search" grand champion, Salta dropped to his knees in disbelief. He said he was feeling "blessed" and so "grateful."
"This is real magic right here," Salta added.

title: Miley Cyrus Announces 'Hannah Montana' 20th Anniversary Special. What to Know
summary: Get ready to have the best of both worlds — again
story: Get ready to have the best of both worlds — again!
On Feb. 17, Miley Cyrus announced that she will be celebrating the 20th anniversary of her breakout show “Hannah Montana” with a television special, “Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special.”
“Going back to where it all began,” Cyrus wrote, adding that the special will premiere this March.

In the accompanying clip, a car with the license plate “HM 20” is seen pulling up to Stage 9 at a studio lot while “The Best of Both Worlds” plays in the background. Then a pair of black heeled boots step out of the Mustang.
Just four days before the announcement, Cyrus had shared throwback photos from her Hannah Montana days on Instagram.
“Going through the archives… so many memories 💖 xoMC,” she captioned the slideshow that included her as the pop star, old scripts and a bedazzled jacket.
Additionally, in the press release for the “Hannah Montana” special, the Grammy winner reflected on what the show meant to her.

“’Hannah Montana’ will always be a part of who I am. What started as a TV show became a shared experience that shaped my life and the lives of so many fans, and I’ll always be thankful for that connection,” she said. “The fact that it still means so much to people all these years later is something I’m very proud of. This ‘Hannahversary’ is my way of celebrating and thanking the fans who’ve stood by me for 20 years.”
Here is everything you need to know about the “Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special.”
When Will the 'Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special' Be Released?
The "Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special" will premiere March 24 on Disney+.

March 24 is the original date the Disney Channel show premiered in 2006. Starring Cyrus, along with her dad Billy Ray Cyrus, Emily Osment, Jason Earles and Mitchel Musso, “Hannah Montana” ran for four seasons between March 2006 and January 2011.
What Will the 'Hannah Montana 20th Anniversary Special’ Be About?
The “Hannah Montana” special will include an intimate interview with Miley Cyrus hosted by Alex Cooper and filmed in front of a live studio audience.
The pair will talk about the creation of the show, her famous character and the lasting impact they have had on fans worldwide.

“With heartfelt nostalgia and fresh perspective, Cyrus will revisit the moments, music and memories that defined an era,” the release states. “Viewers will be treated to never-before-seen archival footage, while some of the most memorable sets from ‘Hannah Montana’ are brought back to life — including the Stewart family living room and the legendary Hannah Montana closet.”
The special also teases “familiar *notes* that find their way back into the spotlight…”
No additional former cast members or special guests have been announced at this time.

title: Mckenna Grace Joins 'Scooby-Doo' Live-Action Netflix Show as Daphne
summary: The "Scooby-Doo" live-action series has found its Daphne.
story: The "Scooby-Doo" live-action series has found its Daphne.
Mckenna Grace has been cast as the stylish red-headed character that forms part of the Scooby Gang. The 19-year-old actor is the first star to land a major role in the upcoming Netflix show.
Grace confirmed the casting news on her Instagram Feb. 17, sharing a screenshot of a Deadline article about the series, as well as a photo of herself as a young child dressed as Daphne Blake.
“Oh my jeepers,” Grace wrote, adding a crying emoji and purple heart. “I can’t believe life is real I could cry all over again just looking at this announcement. So thankful, SO excited.”
She also added a blue car, magnifying glass and another purple heart at the end of her post.
Netflix announced plans to create an eight-episode live-action version of the popular animated series in March 2025.

The show is set to be a "modern reimagining" where "old friends Shaggy and Daphne team up at summer camp with scientific townie Velma and the strange but handsome Fred to solve the mystery of a lonely lost Great Dane puppy — who may have witnessed a supernatural murder," the show's description reads.
Grace is no stranger to the “Scooby-Doo” universe. The actor voiced young Daphne in the 2020 animated film “Scoob!”

Grace most recently starred in "Regretting You" and "Five Nights at Freddy's 2." She's also set to appear in the upcoming "Scream 7" sequel and in "The Hunger Games: Sunrise on the Reaping" as Maysilee Donner.
Now with casting underway for the “Scooby-Doo” series, expect to hear who will join Grace as Shaggy, Velma and Fred next.

title: ‘HIDDEN VALLEY RAAAAANCH’: Why Are People Singing About Salad Dressing Online?
summary: The creator of the viral Hidden Valley Ranch jingle, Alandria Killian (aka @Langguini on TikTok) is officially teaming up with the salad dressing company.
story: Who would have ever thought a jingle about ranch dressing would slap so hard?
A short ditty that evokes the feathered tresses and leather pants of ’80s hair bands — about Hidden Valley Ranch of all things — is TikTok’s latest earworm.
The witty tune has been reposted, covered and sung out loud to no one in particular since its debut, racking up millions of views.
Everyone who posts it mentions how catchy it is: There are those singing it in the office, friends giggling as they sing in the car and a security guard vocalizing it repeatedly.
The song’s lyrics evoke an era when Aqua Net hairspray could make a killing camping outside of a Bon Jovi concert:
Hidden Valley Ranch / Sauce boss, they will find you / Hidden Valley Ranch will guide you / Hot wings: not without you / Hidden Valley Ranch!

The jingle has gone so viral, it’s been used to soundtrack meta-style tributes to its own awesomeness, from spec ads by aspiring brand creatives to nail art to anime, making everything a little better — much like ranch dressing does, its fans would argue.

The creator of the jingle is Charlotte, North Carolina resident Alandria Killian (known as @Langguini on TikTok), who was driven to sing the Foreigner-esque freestyle when she saw that Hidden Valley Ranch’s official account commented on another viral jingle she made about Mountain Dew.

“It popped up in my head immediately,” Killian tells TODAY.com. “A lot of people do not know that that video literally was me seeing the comment, clicking record, singing, ‘Hidden Valley Ranch,’ and boom.”
Killian, who works in the financial industry, says she recorded her first jingle about Mountain Dew as a comedic response to rumors about another TikTok jingle creator: Romeo Bingham.

The TikTok-to-TV crooner was allegedly paid $2 million dollars for their mega-viral Dr Pepper jingle, which has garnered over 138 million views and was made into a commercial by the soda brand. (Dr Pepper tells TODAY.com it cannot disclose the financials behind the partnership or advertisement.)
Now, ranch dressing takes center stage on social media.
There are several creators going viral alongside Killian, and one is balloon artist Lexus Lewis (@vmarievrt), whom people mistake for the original singer due to her seamless lip-synching.

“I had to keep telling people because I was getting tagged in duets and remixes, and I was like, ‘Y’all, please show Langguini some love,’” Lewis tells TODAY.com. “To take credit for anything that’s not mine? It’s Black History Month — we’re not going to do that.”
And then there’s sister singing duo Alarie, who did a viral cover of the song, making sure to add a few signature runs and vocal trills at the end of their acapella.
The sisters, a real singer-songwriter duo, have tracks and performances planned for this year.

“On our ‘personality videos’ where we’re singing silly actually segues people into saying, ‘Hey, these girls can actually sing,’” one-half of the duo, Arielle Brown, tells TODAY.com. “A lot of people have asked if we can put our stuff on streaming platforms, and that just gives us the little segue to tell them that we already are.”
Alanna Brown, the other half of the duo, says, “It’s really cool to be a part of something that everybody loves to use.”

“The jingle took off because it was so catchy and fun — and trust me, we know,” Daniel Schear, Hidden Valley Ranch’s marketing director, tells TODAY.com. “Our social media manager got it stuck in her head, then she got it stuck in mine, and soon the whole team was humming it.”
Killian won’t reveal what she and Hidden Valley are up to, exactly, but confirmed they have been in contact and posted a cryptic message to that effect on Feb. 15.
“Making it official felt like the natural next step, and collaborating with the creator allowed us to formally recognize the talent and creativity that brought the moment to life,” Schear says.
Lewis says she appreciates the brand working with the jingle’s creator and giving her full credit, as well as “reaching out to smaller creatives, especially Black ones.”
She points out the plight of Kayla Newman, the creator of the viral phrase “on fleek.” Brands and other creators used the term without giving her credit.
“People like supporting people, especially if you’re the underdog. That’s why everybody was rooting for Romeo, and now we’re rooting for a Langguini as well, because we want to see people win.”
Only time will tell what they’ve got up their ranch-covered sleeves.

“I’m overwhelmed to see the different variations and the different versions; it’s mostly amazing to see,” Killian says. “It’s Black History Month. Ranch was founded by a Black man (Steve Henson). I’m loving it. Honestly, I’m hoping that the movement continues throughout the entire year.”

title: Where Is 'America's Next Top Model' Winner Dani Evans Now?
summary: “America’s Next Top Model” Season 6 winner Dani Evans was asked to close the gap between her front teeth while filming the show.
story: “America’s Next Top Model” Season 6 winner Dani Evans was asked to close the gap between her front teeth while filming the show. Nearly 20 years later, she’s sharing how she really felt while making the difficult decision.
Evans appears in a “Reality Check: Inside America’s Next Tp Model,” a new docuseries about the former reality TV competition, and looks back on her experience on the show.
In the second episode, Evans recalls how she felt pressured to undergo a dental procedure to continue on with the show.
Below, we’re recapping her “America’s Next Top Model” journey.
When Was Dani Evans On the Show?
Evans appeared in Season 6 of “America’s Next Top Model,” which aired in 2006.
During the season, Evans and her fellow contestant Joanie Sprague were given the opportunity to undergo dental procedures.
Sprague, who had a tooth removed, was initially excited to enhance her smile.
Evans, however, was proud of the gap between her front teeth and declined to correct it at first. “I love the gap. The gap is staying in my mouth. That’s who I am,” she said.
She got pushback. “Do you really think you can have a Covergirl contract with the gap in your mouth?” Tyra Banks asked. She later added, “It’s not marketable.’
She later agreed to undergo the procedure after a near elimination.
What Has Evans Said About Her Time on ‘America’s Next Top Model’?

While appearing in the docuseries “Reality Check: Inside America’s Next Top Model,” Evans recalled her motivation for signing up for the reality competition after finishing high school in Little Rock, Arkansas.
“‘Top Model’ was a one-way ticket for me to get out of my hometown,” she said.
Evans was hesitant to sign up for the show when her brother suggested it.

“I was like, ‘I would f------ never.’ He was like, ‘Why?’ I was like, ‘Cause it’s fake modeling, dude. They humiliate girls on that show.’ And he was like, ‘Girl, this could be a ticket, a free ticket out. We don’t have money to get you to New York,’” she recalled.

Once she was selected for the show, Evans was vocal about her fondness for the gap between her two front teeth. So when she learned that a dentist was available to “perfect” her smile, the model wasn’t sure how to react.

“So we pull up to the dentist and the guy keeps asking me if I want to get anything done to my teeth. I was like, ‘No, I don’t want to close my gap. That’s not your right to tell me what to do with my body,’” she recalled in the docuseries.
Later, the show’s host Tyra Banks questioned Evans and asked why she hadn’t closed her gap, saying it was “not marketable” as a model.

“When Tyra called my name, she was like, ‘So if I decide to keep you, are you going to get your gap closed?’ And then that’s when I was like, ‘So what you’re saying to me is if I don’t get my gap closed you’re going to send me home,’” she recalled.
After calling her mother for advice, Evans agreed to make the change.
“And so I decided to play the game and I got my gap closed some. It’s my life and it was toyed with consciously and me saying no and them going against that is invasive,” she said.
A few seasons later, Banks asked another contestant to create a larger gap between her front teeth. When asked how she reacted to that, Evans said, “Girl, that is absolutely ridiculous.”
In an interview for the docuseries, Banks said she has “apologized” for what happened to Evans.

“That was between a rock and a hard place for me because there were agents that would tell me she will not work with those teeth. It’s just not going to happen. That’s what they told me. And again, I could’ve just been quiet and let them handle it, but hindsight is 20/20 for all of us. It just so happens that a lot of things that are 20/20 for me happened in front of the world,” Banks said.
Evans then appeared and offered the following reaction.
“Bull f------ s---. Me getting my gap closed is not opening any doors for me. You knew what you were doing for the show. You were making good for TV at my expense,” she said.
Where Is Dani Evans Now?
After winning Season 6 of “America’s Next Top Model,” Evans lived in New York and pursued a career in modeling.
In the docuseries, she described moving into an apartment with other aspiring models and feeling like the “black sheep” while dealing with her “cold” modeling agency.
Evans said she learned “very early on” that “America’s Next Top Model” had a “stigma” associated with it within the modeling industry and made it difficult for her to find work.
Evans eventually walked away from the modeling industry, a move she called the “best decision” of her life.
Per Neflix’s Tudum, the former model began writing a memoir and is on a “journey of awakening, both spiritually and emotionally.”

title: Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band Announce ‘Land of Hope and Dreams American Tour’
summary: Bruce Springsteen announced Feb. 17 that he and the E Street Band are launching the ‘Land of Hope and Dreams American Tour.’
story: Bruce Springsteen announced Feb. 17 that he and the E Street Band will launch a new tour across the United States this spring.

The Grammy-winning singer made the announcement in a video he posted on Instagram, sharing that their “Land of Hope and Dreams American Tour” will consist of 20 dates that run from March 21 to May 27.

“Brothers and sisters, fans, friends and good folk from coast to coast, we are living through dark, disturbing and dangerous times, but do not despair. The calvary is coming,” he said at the top of the clip.

“Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band will be taking the stage this spring, from Minneapolis to California to Texas to Washington, D.C, for the ‘Land of Hope and Dreams American Tour,’” he continued, in part.
Springsteen, who has been a vocal critic of President Trump and wrote a song about his ICE policies, said the tour is a chance to honor the values of the country.

“Everyone, regardless of where you stand or what you believe in, is welcome,” he continued. “So come on out and join the united free Republic of E Street Nation for an American spring of rock and rebellion. I’ll see you there.”
This new set of tour dates marks Springsteen and The E Street Band's first shows in North America since 2024.
How to Get Bruce Springsteen Tickets
Tickets for the “Land of Hope and Dreams American Tour” go on sale on Friday, Feb. 20 and Saturday, Feb. 21 between 10 a.m. and 12 p.m.
For ticketing information, please visit BruceSpringsteen.net/Tour.

title: 'Tell Me Lies' Is Over. How That Messy Finale Wraps Up All the Characters' Storylines
summary: What Happens in the Tell Me Lies finale? What comes next for Wrigley and Bree?
story: It’s the official end to a chaotic cycle of mind-games, deception and heartbreak at Baird College and beyond for the “Tell Me Lies” friend group that just kept spiraling.
Creator and executive producer Meaghen Oppenheimer announced Feb. 16 that the third season would be the last, just hours ahead of the series finale.

The end to the Hulu series, based on Carola Lovering’s novel of the same name, finally airs out what each character has secretly been holding onto, from the college betrayals of 2008 straight into Bree (Catherine Missal) and Evan’s (Branden Cook) wedding in 2015.
This time around, we’re looking at everyone sideways, not just Lucy Albright (Grace Van Patten) and Stephen DeMarco (Jackson White).
Spencer House, who plays Wrigley, and Costa D'Angelo, who play Alex, share the same verdict: Not everyone gets their happy ending.
“I would say it’s the most we’ve ever seen s— hit the fan,” House tells TODAY.com.
What does Stephen reveal in his wedding speech?
Stephen makes an explosive speech at Evan and Bree’s wedding, dropping several bombs about his friend group — all set to Britney Spears’ “Toxic.”
Stephen reveals that he and Lucy had hooked up earlier that day and that Bree and Wrigley (Spencer House) had an affair.
He also says Bree was the one to release the video of Lucy admitting to lying about being sexually assaulted. Bree released the video after learning about Evan and Lucy’s hookup.
Where does this leave Wrigley and Bree?
Wrigley and Bree, back in 2009, had a secret affair that ended abruptly. They didn’t talk about it again until reconnecting, and sleeping together, at her engagement party in 2015.
Yes, the same engagement party featured from the very beginning in Season 1, Episode 1.
That means House has kept the Wrigley/Bree relationship storyline a secret for five years.

“It was something that Megan Oppenheimer had let me know in a meeting, basically day one of Tell Me Lies — not day one of shooting, but Day One of just having character meetings,” House told TODAY.com.

“I think with these two characters, you have to lean into the positivity of their good-natured sides because in a show like this, everyone is making horrible decisions and there’s constant shock value. I think that they both have found themselves in a friend group that maybe they don’t match, and there definitely is a spark between them that is more than just shared trauma.”
So, does this mean Wrigley intentionally meant for Stephen to find his phone with calls from Bree?

“I never had a talk with anyone from the creative side on this, like with any writers or with Megan particularly,” House said. “But I assumed that Wrigley was kind of grasping at straws right then and he was like, ‘Oh, wow. Something needs to happen.’ The way I put the phone down, I did think was a little intentional, or I tried to make it seem like that. That was just me playing it that way but, to be honest. Either way, the way it worked out was great.”

While no clear ending for Wrigley and Bree was spelled out in the finale, we do see Bree and Wrigley openly gazing at one another with a smile following the news of their affair, as Evan is actively screaming in Wrigley’s face.
Do Stephen and Lucy end up together?

Kind of? As usual, it did not last. After finding out Bree was the one to “ruin her life” with the tape, she runs away from the wedding with Stephen — who, shortly after, leaves her stranded at a gas station.

It seems like Lucy also loses one of her oldest friendships in the process. Bree told Lucy before she made the decision to leave with Stephen, “We can work out our problems, but if you leave with him right now, this is it.” It can also be left up to interpretation that Lucy is taking a page out of Diana’s book: making Stephen think he won as a means of getting away from him for good.
What happens to the rest of the ‘Tell Me Lies’ characters?
This is the last we’re seeing of the “Tell Me Lies” ensemble. The finale wraps up a few loose ends from 2009:

Lucy gets expelled from Baird after tape of her admitting to lie about being sexually assaulted goes viral across campus.Wrigley gets Stephen kicked out of Yale. He learns, from Pippa (Sonia Mena), that Stephen had sent Diana’s father nudes. Wrigley shares the information to Yale. Diana (Alicia Crowder) ends up going to Yale.Pippa comes out to Wrigley and he is supportive.Conniving professor Oliver (Tom Ellis) never gets his comeuppance. Instead, he and his wife gaslight Bree’s mom into believing that she had made up the affair.
Lucy gets expelled from Baird after tape of her admitting to lie about being sexually assaulted goes viral across campus.

Wrigley gets Stephen kicked out of Yale. He learns, from Pippa (Sonia Mena), that Stephen had sent Diana’s father nudes. Wrigley shares the information to Yale. Diana (Alicia Crowder) ends up going to Yale.
Pippa comes out to Wrigley and he is supportive.
Conniving professor Oliver (Tom Ellis) never gets his comeuppance. Instead, he and his wife gaslight Bree’s mom into believing that she had made up the affair.
Alex (Costa D’Angelo) ends his relationship with Lucy after the tape is released. Alex and Bree were in a difficult foster care home situation together.

“We know Alex grew up in a place where he himself was assaulted, and he was trying to protect Bree, and that’s something to him that’s influenced the rest of his life. It’s the reason he is the way he is. So for someone like Lucy that he starts to fall in love with and he’s intoxicated by — to hear about her lying, that’s something he can’t deal with,” D’Angelo told TODAY.
His story is left open-ended.

“Not everyone gets a happy ending, and we don’t really know what happens to Alex afterwards. We see Alex in the place where he started with Bree, looking after her. That’s all he was doing as a kid and now, like, 10 years later, he’s an adult and he’s doing the same thing,” he said.

If anyone comes out with a happy ending, it’s Pippa and Diana. This pair knows when to make their exit, and strategically. They leave the wedding hand-in-hand as soon as the chaos ensues following Stephen’s speech.

title: Sherri Shepherd Tells Jenna, Sheinelle She's 'Trying to Keep' Show Going After Cancelation Announcement
summary: Sherri Shepherd talked about "Sherri" being canceled while appearing on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle.
story: Sherri Shepherd’s daytime talk show may have been canceled, but she’s not quite ready to walk away from it.
Shepherd said she has been touched by the response from her fans about the cancelation of “Sherri” after four seasons.
“The love has been overwhelming,” she said Feb. 17 on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle.

“I’ve had famous people call me. So many people have called and written to tell me to tell me how much of an impact this show made, and I’m thankful that I got to live a dream for four years. How many people can say that they got to live a dream with great ratings? Where you got to have a platform that made a difference?”
For Shepherd, the goal of the program was always simple.

“I told my staff and crew, the only thing the mandate is we got to give people joy through laughter,” she said. “That’s it. And I would always pray, ‘Lord, show them that side of you that loves to laugh.’ And I got to do that for four years.”
Shepherd, who was nominated in 2023 for a Daytime Emmy Award for outstanding daytime talk show series host, isn’t exactly willing to sit idly by while “Sherri” ends.

“Let me tell you something. I’m from the Shepherds of Chicago. We don’t go down without a fight, not at all. So, I am still really trying to keep this going and you haven’t seen the last of ‘Sherri,’” she said.
Shepherd remains full of gratitude for her fans who’ve supported her during her show’s run.
“I just wanted to thank people for sticking in there with me these four seasons,” she said. “Of course, I’m disappointed, because I thought I finally hit my stride and I was doing good.”
Shepherd can look back fondly at her tenure and continue to pledge to keep her show afloat.

“I mean, I got to dance with former first lady Michelle Obama, you know, and people were having fun and wanted to come on the show. So I’m disappointed,” she said. “I got a great staff and crew that made it look easy. So that’s why I’m saying I’m not going down without a fight.”
Shepherd addressed “Sherri’s” cancelation during an episode of earlier this month.

"I want to say, from the bottom of my heart, I want to thank you for welcoming me into your homes every single day. I want to thank you for laughing and crying with us," she said, while adding the show "was built on joy."
According to Variety, the show will air its final episodes in the fall.

title: Academy-Award Winning Actor Robert Duvall Dead at 95
summary: Academy Award-winning actor Robert Duvall has died at the age of 95, his wife, Luciani Duvall, revealed Feb. 16 in a Facebook post.
story: Academy Award-winning actor Robert Duvall has died, his wife, Luciana Duvall, revealed Feb. 16 in a Facebook post. He was 95.
“Yesterday we said goodbye to my beloved husband, cherished friend, and one of the greatest actors of our time. Bob passed away peacefully at home, surrounded by love and comfort,” she began.

“To the world, he was an Academy Award-winning actor, a director, a storyteller. To me, he was simply everything. His passion for his craft was matched only by his deep love for characters, a great meal, and holding court. For each of his many roles, Bob gave everything to his characters and to the truth of the human spirit they represented.

In doing so, he leaves something lasting and unforgettable to us all. Thank you for the years of support you showed Bob and for giving us this time and privacy to celebrate the memories he leaves behind.”

Last month, Duvall shared a video his wife created in honor of his 95th birthday that featured clips of him over the years, beginning as a little boy and ending with him eating a cookie as an older man.
"Luciana surprised me with this wonderful birthday video in preparation for tomorrow’s big 95! Thank you all for the support all of these years," he wrote.

Duvall’s career began with an appearance in a 1960 episode of the TV series “The Robert Herridge Theater.” He appeared in multiple TV shows before he made his film debut as Boo Radley in the 1962 adaptation of the book “To Kill a Mockingbird,” which won three Oscars and would be the first of a long line of Oscar-nominated and winning movies featuring Duvall.

Duvall won his Academy Award for best actor in 1984 for “Tender Mercies.” He would be nominated seven other times, including his first for “The Godfather,” playing Tom Hagen, the adopted son of Marlon Brando's Don Vito Corleone. He would reprise the role in “The Godfather Part II.”

Duvall also earned Oscar nominations for “Apocalypse Now,” “The Great Santini,” “The Apostle” — which he directed — “A Civil Action” and “The Judge.” In all, he earned Oscar nominations in four different decades. He also starred in “M*A*S*H,” “The Conversation,” “Network,” “The Natural,” “Falling Down,” “Days of Thunder,” “Colors,” “Deep Impact” and “Gone in 60 Seconds.”
Duvall’s final film appearance was in 2022’s “The Pale Blue Eye.” His final onscreen credit was earlier this year in an episode of “The Culture Mafia.”

title: Kelly Clarkson Reflects on Past ‘The Voice’ Wins Ahead of Season 29: Exclusive
summary: Ahead of her grand return to “The Voice” for the show’s special “Battle of Champions” season, Kelly Clarkson looks back at her four past wins and unforgettable moments with Blake Shelton and Adam Levine.
story: Ahead of her grand return to “The Voice” for the show’s special “Battle of Champions” season, Kelly Clarkson is reflecting on her four past wins and unforgettable moments with Blake Shelton and Adam Levine.

For its 29th season, “The Voice” is switching up its format as only three coaches who have all previously won the show — Clarkson, Levine and John Legend — will be on the coaching panel for the first time in the competition’s history. In addition to seeing how the new changes impact the competition, fans are also looking forward to watching Clarkson, who hasn’t been on “The Voice” since Season 23, reclaim her spinning red chair.
In a TODAY.com exclusive clip previewing the season, Clark discusses returning for her 10th season and reminisces about her early days on the show.

“I’m so stoked to come back to ‘The Voice.’ Let’s take a look back at some of my favorite moments,” she says at the start of the video. Her trip down memory lane begins with Season 14, her first on the show. Clarkson pulled off an impressive victory when she won with then-15-year-old Brynn Cartelli, who was also the Grammy winner’s first-ever chair turn.

The following season, Clarkson won again, this time with another teenage phenom named Chevel Shepherd. Speaking about her back-to-back victories, Clarkson says, “I love working with young artists. I just think it’s fun because they’re still so creative and still, like, ready to try anything.”
In Season 21, Clarkson made history when her artist Girl Named Tom, made up of three siblings, became the first trio to win.

But, the “Since U Been Gone” singer reveals that her “most exciting” win was when Season 17 country music singer Jake Hoot made it all the way to the end. She says besting Shelton that season with a country artist made the win even better.

“He saw me when Blake was here. He saw me, and he picked me, and I love him. And then I took him to the winner’s circle. What? That’s right, Shelton!” Clarkson adds while playfully poking fun at the show’s “King of Country.”
She then watches a snippet from her first season on “The Voice” where Shelton mocked her for being inexperienced.

“I love seeing Blake say that I have zero experience on the show, and then I totally won,” she says and laughs. “There’s nothing better. The ultimate revenge of someone just thinking you can’t do something, and then you show them with a trophy.”
Reflecting more on her first season, Clarkson shares that she instantly felt welcomed into “The Voice” family.

She continues, “It was just a really good time. It was a fun experience. And, I give Shelton a lot of crap, but he did help make me feel at home right off the bat, with his brotherly-love jabs. Adam as well, because we had toured together. So I kind of knew people, too, coming in.”
Although Shelton departed “The Voice” after Season 23, Clarkson will soon reunite with Levine for “The Voice: Battle of Champions,” premiering Monday, Feb. 23 on NBC.

For Season 29, past contestants are returning for a second chance to claim “The Voice” trophy. The season will also feature many new twists, including the Triple Turn Competition, a Super Steal, an In-Season All-Star Competition and new voting rules.

title: Former Prince Andrew arrested following Epstein files revelations
summary: Police arrested Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor on Thursday following weeks of revelations over the former prince's friendship and dealings with Jeffrey Epstein.
story: LONDON — Police arrested the former Prince Andrew on Thursday on suspicion of misconduct while in office, following weeks of new revelations over his relationship with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
He was released from police custody 11 hours later "under investigation," Thames Valley Police said in a later statement, meaning he has neither been charged nor exonerated.
Mountbatten-Windsor was seen being driven away from Aylsham Police Station in Norfolk, roughly 50 miles from the Sandringham Estate where he now lives.

The stunning development marks the first arrest of a member of the royal family in centuries and makes him the most high-profile figure to face criminal accusations in a scandal that continues to sweep in some of the world’s richest and most powerful.

The arrest comes after a remarkable fall from grace for the brother of King Charles III, who was stripped of his titles last year and is now known only as Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor — and is yet another blow to Britain's already beleaguered royal family. The king expressed his “deepest concern” at the news Thursday and stressed that “the law must take its course.”
Follow live coverage
Mountbatten-Windsor, who turned 66 on Thursday, has always denied any wrongdoing in connection with Epstein.

The Thames Valley Police said in a statement early Thursday it had arrested a man in his 60s on suspicion of misconduct in public office, which carries a maximum sentence of life imprisonment in the United Kingdom.

title: Ex-South Korean president found guilty of insurrection over martial law order
summary: Yoon Suk Yeol, the impeached former president of South Korea, was found guilty of insurrection and sentenced to life in prison on Thursday over his failed attempt to impose martial law on the U.S. ally
story: Yoon Suk Yeol, the impeached former president of South Korea, was found guilty of insurrection and sentenced to life in prison on Thursday over his failed attempt to impose martial law on the U.S. ally.

The highly anticipated ruling, delivered by a Seoul court, was broadcast across the nation. Prosecutors had asked for the death penalty for Yoon, whose short-lived power grab sent the Asian democracy into political turmoil.
The verdict and sentence were handed down by a three-judge panel at Seoul’s Central District Court, where Yoon’s supporters and critics gathered amid heightened security.

Yoon, 65, had pleaded not guilty to insurrection, the most serious of a range of charges he faces in connection with his 2024 martial law order. Prosecutors had asked for the death penalty in the case.

The court also found Yoon had subverted the constitutional order and abused his authority by ordering troops to storm parliament and arrest certain individuals including Lee Jae Myung, the liberal opposition leader at the time who is now South Korea’s president.
In a statement after the verdict, lawyers for Yoon criticized the trial as “nothing more than a mere formality to reach a predetermined conclusion.” Yoon has the right to appeal.

Facing rulings alongside him were seven former military officers and senior police officials accused of participating in the imposition of martial law, including former Defense Minister Kim Yong-hyun, with prosecutors seeking prison terms of 10 years to life. Kim was also found guilty of insurrection and sentenced to 30 years in prison.
National Assembly Speaker Woo Won-shik, whom Yoon had also targeted for arrest, said after the verdict that Yoon “should now acknowledge his wrongdoing and offer a sincere apology to the people.”
“Now is the time to stop deepening division and conflict in our society through claims that deny or distort the fundamental order of our democratic republic,” Woo said.

Yoon’s martial law order, the first of its kind in South Korea in more than 40 years, shocked a country that became one of Asia’s most vibrant democracies after having spent decades under military-authoritarian rule. South Korea was mired in months of political uncertainty as the chaos from the decree was followed by Yoon’s impeachment and a power vacuum at the top of government.

The episode has also deeply divided the politically polarized public, with Yoon’s conservative supporters cheering his attempts to fight impeachment and arrest in an echo of scenes in the United States. On Thursday, hundreds of Yoon supporters stood outside the court watching the proceedings on a screen, while critics of Yoon also gathered at a protest nearby.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google

The crisis began in December 2024 with Yoon’s surprise late-night announcement in a nationally televised address that he was suspending civilian government in South Korea, including a ban on all political activity and censorship of the news media.

Yoon, who was elected president in 2022, said the martial law order was necessary because “anti-state” forces in the opposition-controlled parliament had paralyzed the government through budget cuts and efforts to impeach multiple senior officials.

The order did not last long, however, as lawmakers rushed to the National Assembly in dramatic overnight scenes, pushing past troops sent there by Yoon and voting unanimously against it in an emergency session. Yoon lifted the order about six hours after he imposed it.

Lawmakers impeached Yoon about 10 days later, and in January 2025 he became South Korea’s first president to be arrested while in office. South Korea’s Constitutional Court upheld his impeachment in April.

Yoon, a former prosecutor, also faces eight criminal trials over the martial law order and other allegations, and he was sentenced to five years in prison last month in the first of those verdicts. He is appealing that ruling.

Other trials are still ongoing, including one in which he is charged with treason after he was accused of ordering that drones be sent into North Korean airspace to provoke a confrontation that could justify martial law.

Yoon denies wrongdoing, saying that he had the right as president to declare martial law and that the order was a short-term, symbolic effort to raise public awareness of the threat from opposition lawmakers.

Prosecutors in the insurrection trial said that the martial law order was a long-planned effort to extend Yoon’s rule indefinitely in violation of the constitution and that he was driven by a “lust for power.”

Other officials from Yoon’s administration have also received prison terms over their roles in carrying out the martial law order. Former Prime Minister Han Duck-soo, 76, was sentenced to 23 years last month, while former Interior Minister Lee Sang-min, 61, was sentenced to seven years last week.

President Lee said ahead of the verdict Thursday that his country was “a model for human history,” noting reports this week that the people of South Korea had been nominated for the Nobel Peace Prize for resisting the martial law order.

It’s possible that Yoon could get a presidential pardon one day, said Bong Young Shik, a visiting professor in the Graduate School of International Studies at Yonsei University in Seoul. He pointed to the case of former President Chun Doo-hwan, who was also sentenced to life in prison for insurrection in 1996 but was later freed.

But that was a long time ago, Bong said, and public sentiment in South Korea — where democratic values have become deeply embedded in recent decades — “may not be so forgiving for President Yoon this time.”

It will take time to “close the wound” created by the martial law episode given the divisions around it, Bong said. But ironically, he added, the fierce support for Yoon in some quarters “testifies to the maturity and strength of South Korean democracy.”

“We are going to live with diverse voices, some of which may be very difficult to accept by people with different political ideologies and opinions,” Bong said. “But South Korean society will continue to exist with a variety of competing visions about how the country should be.”

title: Robots and Draco Malfoy: How China is celebrating the Year of the Horse
summary: BEIJING — The Lunar New Year is coming, and China is on the move
story: BEIJING — The Lunar New Year is coming, and China is on the move.

Hundreds of millions of people are traveling to their hometowns or making overseas trips ahead of the Feb. 17 holiday, known in China as Spring Festival, in what is considered the world’s largest annual human migration.

They will make a record 9.5 billion trips during the 40-day period around the holiday, up from 9.02 billion last year, according to the National Development and Reform Commission. That includes 540 million trips by train and 95 million by air, with the vast majority traveling by road.

For many workers living far from home, it’s the one chance they get each year to take a break and spend time with family. This year’s festival, which ushers in the Year of the Horse in the Chinese zodiac, starts Sunday with nine days of public holiday, one more than usual.
Xu Zhanyu, 41, a finance worker in Beijing whose hometown of Tangshan is about 100 miles away, said that although he can visit “any time,” Spring Festival is special.
“The biggest thing is the family reunion,” he said in an interview  Wednesday outside the bustling Beijing Railway Station.
“It’s the only time you can let go of everything and just be with the people you love and those who love you. You don’t have to think about anything else.”

title: Why a dart frog poison believed to have killed Alexei Navalny points to the Kremlin
summary: Precise, deniable and, in Russia’s case, grimly familiar.
story: Precise, deniable and, in Russia’s case, grimly familiar. Poison has become less a medieval cliché than a geopolitical signature flourish in recent years.

Accusations of Russian poisoning surfaced again this week after Western governments said laboratory analysis found the rare frog-derived toxin epibatidine, a compound associated with Ecuadorian poison dart frogs, in samples from the body of Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny.

The allegation feeds into a long and deeply contested narrative around high-profile poison cases in Vladimir Putin’s Russia, from radioactive tea to nerve agents. Moscow has consistently denied involvement in the episodes, which have shaped its global reputation.

A joint statement by the U.K., France, Germany, Sweden and the Netherlands on Saturday said forensic tests concluded that epibatidine, a powerful neurotoxin, was present in Navalny’s system after his death in a Siberian penal colony in 2024.

Russia’s prison service reported in February 2024 that Navalny, 47, died after having felt unwell following a walk around the high-security facility in a remote town above the Arctic Circle where he was serving a combined 30½-year jail sentence.

British officials said only the Russian government had the capability and opportunity to deploy the toxin against Navalny, and they have reported the case to the Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons. French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot said that the episode shows Vladimir Putin is willing to use chemical agents against his own citizens to maintain power.
U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said at a news conference Sunday he is aware of the “troubling” findings and has no reason to question them.
Russian Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Maria Zakharova said Saturday that Russia would comment “where there are test results, where there are formulas of the substances.”

While Russia dismisses the allegations, the case has focused attention on the extraordinary lethality of the substance involved, an exotic toxin whose potency and synthetic accessibility make it a weapon that experts say can be produced and deployed only by a state with advanced chemical capabilities.

Epipedobates anthonyi, known as Anthony’s poison arrow frog, typically measures 22 mm long. Its skin carries sufficient epibatidine to kill a human several times over, with lethal doses measured in minuscule amounts as little as 1.4 micrograms.
The drug “is not naturally found in Russia,” the British foreign ministry said in a joint statement Saturday, but its absence in nature is irrelevant when a state is able to reproduce and deploy it.

“The structure is known and it’s possible to synthesize it chemically, so you wouldn’t have to go to Ecuador looking for brightly colored frogs, wash them down and get the toxin off their skin,” Alastair Hay, a professor of environmental toxicology at the University of Leeds, told NBC News.
“You could make it in the lab,” he said.

title: An artist, a geophysicist and a fruit seller: Accounts of Iran's brutal crackdown emerge
summary: With Iran still reeling from a brutal crackdown on protests, details about victims are trickling out and the world is gradually getting a clearer picture of the violence used.
story: A fruit seller and father of two killed during his first protest. A biotechnology graduate with a passion for art who bled to death in her father’s arms. A distraught family ordered to pay morgue officials $7,000 for a loved one’s body unless they lie and say their relative died at the hands of anti-government rioters.

These are a tiny fraction of the thousands of Iranians killed or wounded when the government cracked down on protests a month ago.
